topic: Lecture 1: Introduction to Feedbacks and Control System
reviewerFacts: Control Systems Engineering focuses on the implementation of control systems mainly derived by mathematical modeling of a diverse set of systems.
It is an interdisciplinary field that deals with control systems, applying control theory to design systems with desired behavior.
A Control System is a device or set of devices that manages, commands, directs, or regulates the behavior of other devices or systems.
Sensors are used in practice to measure the output of the process being controlled, providing corrective feedback to achieve desired performance.
The 1st feedback control device was Ktesibios's water clock, invented in 300 BCE by Greek Engineer Ctesibius of Alexandria in Egypt.
In 1620, Drebbel invented the thermostat for the incubation of eggs using mercury.
The Industrial Revolution (1769) was a period of major technological and economic change that transformed societies from agrarian to industrial.
In 1788, Watt's Centrifugal Flyball Governor was used for the speed regulation of steam engines.
In 1793, Breguet (a watchmaker) invented the Pendule Sympathique, a closed-loop feedback system to synchronize pocket watches.
Up to the Industrial Revolution, the design of feedback control systems was based on Trial-and-Error and a great deal of Engineering Intuition, making it 'More of an ART than science'.

The Birth of Mathematical Control Theory happened in 1868 when James Clark Maxwell's paper 'On Governors' explained the instabilities of the flyball governor using Differential Equations (Time-Domain).
James Clark Maxwell is considered the Father of Control Theory because his work is the first significant work to mathematically analyze a control system.
In 1877, E. J. Routh provided a numerical technique to determine stable roots called the Routh Stability Criterion.
In 1895, A. Hurwitz provided his own stability criterion. Both Routh and Hurwitz's works contributed to the Routh-Hurwitz Criterion.
In the late 19th Century, Euler, Laplace, Heaviside, and Fourier developed different methods for mathematical modeling.
In the early 20th Century, two important occurrences related to control theory were the Development of the telephone and mass communications, and the World Wars.
Frequency Domain Analysis (developed by Fourier, Laplace, Cauchy, and others) was heavily used in communication systems.
WW1 was characterized by the maturation of the battleship and the introduction of submarines.
In 1910, Sperry invented the Gyroscope for the stabilization & steering of ships.
WW2 saw the dominance of aircraft carriers, radar-guided weaponry, and specialized amphibious vessels.
Prior to WWII (1922), N. Minorsky introduced the PID Controller, his 3-term controller for ship steering.
In 1934, Hazen coined 'Servomechanism', which implies a master/slave relationship in systems.
In the aftermath of WWII (1947), N. B. Nichols developed the Nichols Chart for the design of feedback systems.
In 1948, W. R. Evans presented his Root Locus technique providing a direct way to locate closed-loop poles in the s-plane.
During the 1950s (Laplace Domain), much controls work was focused on the s-plane and obtaining a desirable closed-loop Step Response.

1950s milestones include the theory of sampled data systems, computer-controlled oil refinery at Port Arthur, and the development of Nuclear Reactors which motivated works in industrial process control.
The Space Age began in 1957 when the Soviet Union launched Sputnik (the world's 1st artificial satellite), giving rise to activities in the USA on automatic control design.
During the Space Age, systems became advance non-linear Multivariable, making design impossible in the frequency-domain, resulting in a Return to Time Domain techniques.
In 1960, major developments independently occurred in the theory of communication & control (Time-domain), including the Inertial Navigation System.
Under Optimal Control in 1960, the Kalman Filter was developed.
In Nonlinear Control Theory in the 1960s, the US extended the work of Popov and Lyapunov in nonlinear stability.
Digital Computer Timeline: 1830 (Babbage introduced modern computer principle), 1948 (Neumann directed construction of IAS stored program), 1960 (2nd Gen. Computers using solid-state tech).
Digital Computer Timeline cont.: 1965 (DEC built PDP-8, beginning of Microcomputer industry), 1969 (Hoff invented the microprocessor).
Computers have two main purposes in Modern Control: Solve Matrix Design Equations and Implement modern control & filtering schemes on actual systems.
In 1970 (Information Age), Astrom et. al. established digital control in process applications. The era was defined by 'Solve problem using computer'.
In 1960, R. F. Kalman became associated with the State Space Model under Modern Control Theory.
In 1974, the application of digital filtering theory was investigated.
In 1983, the introduction of PC-made design of modern control systems made it possible for individual engineers to use software like MATLAB.
Control Theory deals with the control of dynamical systems in engineered processes and machines.

questions.question: What is Control System Engineering? Focus on implementation of control systems mainly derived by ________ of a diverse set of systems.
questions.explanation: Based on the presentation, Control System Engineering focuses on implementation mainly derived by mathematical modeling of a diverse set of systems.

questions.question: Control Systems Engineering is an interdisciplinary field that deals with control systems, applying ________ to design system with desired behavior.
questions.explanation: The slide defines it as an interdisciplinary field applying control theory to design systems with desired behavior.

questions.question: What is a device or set of devices that manages, commands, directs, or regulates the behavior of other devices or systems?
questions.explanation: This is the exact definition of a Control System provided in the slides.

questions.question: In practice, ________ are used to measure the output of the process being controlled.
questions.explanation: The presentation states that sensors are used to measure the output of the process being controlled.

questions.question: What do sensors provide to achieve desired performance?
questions.explanation: Sensors provide corrective feedback to achieve desired performance.

questions.question: What is considered the 1st feedback control device invented in 300 BCE?
questions.explanation: Ktesibios's water clock is identified as the 1st feedback control device invented in 300 BCE.

questions.question: Who invented the 1st feedback control device (water clock) in Alexandria, Egypt?
questions.explanation: It was invented by Greek Engineer Ctesibius of Alexandria in Egypt.

questions.question: In 1620, Drebbel invented the ________ for the incubation of eggs using mercury.
questions.explanation: Drebbel invented the thermostat for the incubation of eggs using mercury in 1620.

questions.question: What period of major technological and economic change began in the 18th century and transformed societies from agrarian to industrial?
questions.explanation: The Industrial Revolution in 1769 was a period of major technological and economic change that transformed societies from agrarian to industrial.

questions.question: What device was used for speed regulation of steam engine in 1788?
questions.explanation: Watt's Centrifugal Flyball Governor was used for speed regulation of the steam engine in 1788.

questions.question: In 1793, Breguet (a watchmaker) invented the Pendule Sympathique. What is it?
choices: An open-loop system to regulate pressure
A closed-loop feedback system to synchronize pocket watches
A mechanical calculator for mathematical modeling
questions.correctAnswer: A closed-loop feedback system to synchronize pocket watches
questions.explanation: Pendule Sympathique is a closed-loop feedback system to synchronize pocket watches.

questions.question: Design of feedback control systems up to the Industrial Revolution was by ________ + great deal of Engineering Intuition.
questions.explanation: The design of feedback control systems up to the Industrial Revolution was by Trial-and-Error and a great deal of Engineering Intuition.

questions.question: Up to the Industrial Revolution, the design of feedback control systems was considered "More of an ________ than science."
questions.explanation: The slides explicitly state it was "More of an ART than science".

questions.question: Who explained the instabilities of the flyball governor using Differential Equations in an 1868 paper?
questions.explanation: James Clark Maxwell's 1868 paper "On Governors" explained the instabilities of the flyball governor using Differential Equations.

questions.question: Who is considered the Father of Control Theory?
questions.explanation: James Clark Maxwell is considered the Father of Control Theory.

questions.question: Why is Maxwell considered the Father of Control Theory?
choices: His work is the first significant work to mathematically analyze a control system.
questions.correctAnswer: His work is the first significant work to mathematically analyze a control system.
questions.explanation: He is considered the Father of Control Theory because his work is the first significant work to mathematically analyze a control system.

questions.question: What does Maxwell's 1868 paper represent in the evolution timeline?
questions.explanation: The timeline points to 1868 and Maxwell's work as the "Birth of Mathematical Control Theory".

questions.question: In 1877, E. J. Routh provided a numerical technique to determine stable roots. What is this called?
questions.explanation: E. J. Routh provided the Routh Stability Criterion to determine stable roots.

questions.question: In 1895, who provided his own stability criterion that, together with Routh's, contributed to the Routh-Hurwitz Criterion?
questions.explanation: A. Hurwitz provides his own stability criterion in 1895, contributing to the Routh-Hurwitz Criterion.

questions.question: In the Late 19th Century, who among the following developed different methods for mathematical modeling?
questions.explanation: Euler, Laplace, Heaviside & Fourier developed different methods for mathematical modeling in the late 19th Century.

questions.question: What were the two important occurrences related to control theory in the Early 20th Century?
choices: Invention of computers and the Space Age
Development of the telephone and mass communications, and World Wars
Invention of the thermostat and the steam engine
questions.correctAnswer: Development of the telephone and mass communications, and World Wars
questions.explanation: The early 20th century saw the development of the telephone and mass communications, and the World Wars.

questions.question: ________ developed by Fourier, Laplace, Cauchy and others were used in communication systems.
questions.explanation: Frequency Domain Analysis was developed and used in communication systems.

questions.question: WW1 was characterized by the maturation of the battleship and the introduction of submarines. In 1910, what did Sperry invent for stabilization & steering of ships?
questions.explanation: In 1910, Sperry invented the Gyroscope for stabilization & steering of ships.

questions.question: In 1922 (Pre WWII), N. Minorsky introduced his 3-term controller for ship steering. What is this controller called?
questions.explanation: N. Minorsky introduced the PID Controller (his 3-term controller) for ship steering in 1922.

questions.question: In 1934, Hazen coined the term ________ which implies a master/slave relationship in systems.
questions.explanation: Hazen coined "Servomechanism" which implies a master/slave relationship in systems.

questions.question: In the aftermath of WWII (1947), N. B. Nichols developed the ________ for the design of feedback systems.
questions.explanation: N. B. Nichols developed the Nichols Chart for the design of feedback systems.

questions.question: In 1948, W. R. Evans presented his ________ technique providing a direct way to locate closed-loop poles in s-plane.
questions.explanation: W. R. Evans presented the Root Locus technique to locate closed-loop poles.

questions.question: During the 1950s (Laplace Domain), much controls work was focused on s-plane and obtaining desirable closed-loop ________.
questions.explanation: The slide explicitly mentions work was focused on the s-plane and obtaining desirable closed-loop Step Response.

questions.question: What event in 1957 marked the start of the Space Age and gave rise to activities in the USA on automatic control design?
questions.explanation: In 1957, the Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the world's 1st artificial satellite, sparking control design activities in the USA.

questions.question: Why did control design return to time domain techniques during the Space Age?
choices: Because frequency domain analysis was too expensive.
Because design of control system for advance non-linear multivariable systems is impossible in the freq-domain.
Because computers could not process Laplace transforms.
Because the Soviet Union banned frequency domain methods.
questions.correctAnswer: Because design of control system for advance non-linear multivariable systems is impossible in the freq-domain.
questions.explanation: The slide notes that design for advance non-linear multivariable systems is impossible in the frequency-domain, thus turning back to time domain techniques.

questions.question: In 1960, major development independently occurred in the theory of communication & control in what domain?
questions.explanation: Major development independently occurred in the theory of communication & control in the time-domain.

questions.question: For Navigation in 1960, what system was developed?
questions.explanation: Navigation developments in 1960 included the Inertial Navigation System.

questions.question: Under Optimal Control in 1960, what specific filter was developed?
questions.explanation: Optimal Control saw the development of the Kalman Filter.

questions.question: In Nonlinear Control Theory in the 1960s, the US extended the work of Popov and ________ in nonlinear stability.
questions.explanation: The US extended the work of Popov and Lyapunov in nonlinear stability.

questions.question: In the history of the digital computer, who introduced the modern computer principle in 1830?
questions.explanation: Babbage introduced the modern computer principle in 1830.

questions.question: Who directed the construction of the IAS stored program in 1948?
questions.explanation: Neumann directed the construction of the IAS stored program in 1948.

questions.question: What type of technology did the 2nd Generation Computers use in 1960?
questions.explanation: 1960 2nd Gen. Computers used solid-state tech.

questions.question: What did Hoff invent in 1969?
questions.explanation: Hoff invented the microprocessor in 1969.

questions.question: What are the two main purposes of Computers in Modern Control?
choices: Solve Matrix Design Equations & Implement modern control & filtering schemes on actual system
Plot Root Locus and draw Nichols Charts
questions.correctAnswer: Solve Matrix Design Equations & Implement modern control & filtering schemes on actual system
questions.explanation: The two main purposes are to Solve Matrix Design Equations and Implement modern control & filtering schemes on actual systems.

questions.question: In the 1950s, developments included the theory of sampled data systems and computer-controlled oil refinery at ________.
questions.explanation: The slide mentions a computer-controlled oil refinery at Port Arthur.

questions.question: The 1950s motivated works in industrial process control & instrumentation partly due to the development of ________.
questions.explanation: Development of Nuclear Reactors motivated works in industrial process control & instrumentation.

questions.question: In 1970, Astrom et. al. established ________ in process applications.
questions.explanation: Astrom et. al. established the digital control in process applications.

questions.question: What theory's application was investigated in 1974?
questions.explanation: Application of digital filtering theory was investigated in 1974.

questions.question: What was introduced in 1983 that made the design of modern control systems possible for individual engineers?
questions.explanation: Introduction of PC-made design of modern control systems made it possible for individual engineers.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of control system software developed in the Information Age?
questions.explanation: The slide lists ProgramCC, Easy5, MATRIX, SIMNON, and MATLAB as control system softwares developed.

questions.question: According to the "Classical & Modern Control Theory" timeline, what model is associated with R. F. Kalman in 1960?
questions.explanation: R. F. Kalman is associated with the State Space Model in 1960 (Modern Control Theory).

questions.question: According to the timeline, what was the primary approach in 1920 (Classical Control Theory)?
choices: Application & Development of math methods in Frequency Domain
questions.correctAnswer: Application & Development of math methods in Frequency Domain
questions.explanation: In 1920, the approach was the Application & Development of math methods in Frequency Domain (Nyquist, Nichols, Root Locus, Bode Plot).

questions.question: Control Theory deals with the control of ________ in engineered processes and machines.
questions.explanation: Slide 31 states Control Theory deals with control of dynamical systems in engineered processes and machines.

questions.question: Classical Control Theory relies on tools like Nyquist, Nichols, Root Locus, and ________.
questions.explanation: The timeline lists Nyquist, Nichols, Root Locus, and Bode Plot under the 1920 Frequency Domain section.

questions.question: During the 1970s (Information Age), control theory evolved to ________.
questions.explanation: The timeline for 1970 (Information Age) explicitly says "Solve problem using computer".
